pr.title: Match some of gricemt.c and grpura.c
pr.author: MarioLover63

summary: Matched substantial portions of the Ice Mountain stage file gricemt.c and the Pura/Pokefloats-related stage file grpura.c, replacing many placeholder stubs and UNK prototypes with typed stage initialization, callback, animation, ground, camera, material, and item/enemy-generator code. The PR also added supporting ground-variable structs and helper prototypes in gr/types.h, gricemt.h, grpura.h, grzakogenerator.h, and mplib.h. Review evidence is sparse, but the merge and maintainer comment were positive.
what_changed: Expanded src/melee/gr/gricemt.c from mostly placeholders into many typed Ice Mountain routines, including grIm_803E4800 StageData, grIm_803E4718 StageCallbacks, global stage data, initialization/teardown paths, gobj creation through Ground_801C14D0, GX/proc setup, JObj lookup/translation, animation setup, material callbacks, and adventure-mode grZakoGenerator handling.

Expanded src/melee/gr/grpura.c from stubs into a stage implementation with grPu_803E6800 StageCallbacks, grPu_803E6A3C StageData for /GrPu.dat, background/color globals, image/toon texture data, stage init, camera-bound adjustment, gobj creation, animation setup, material/toon handling, and update/destruction callbacks.

Replaced many gricemt.h UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with typed signatures using void, bool, HSD_GObj/Ground_GObj, HSD_JObj, Ground, Vec3, DynamicsDesc, mpLib_GroundEnum, and float/int parameters where known.
Added IceMountainParams in gricemt.h with fields through xCC, including HSD_GObj* xAC and bool xBC, to type the parameter block returned by Ground_801C49F8 and used by Ice Mountain code.

Added grIceMt_GroundVars details and a new grIceMt_GroundVars2 in gr/types.h, replacing a small padding-only Ice Mountain variable layout with named offset fields including HSD_GObj*, s16, f32, void* array, and HSD_JObj* members.
Added grPura_GroundVars and grPura_GroundVars2 to the Ground union in gr/types.h for Pura-specific state, including color/index fields and HSD_JObj* pointers.
Updated grpura.h prototypes from placeholder/unknown signatures to HSD_GObj*, HSD_JObj*, HSD_DObj*, DynamicsDesc*, Vec3*, bool, int, and void signatures for functions touched by the match.

Adjusted grzakogenerator.h prototypes for grZakoGenerator_801CAE04 and grZakoGenerator_801CAEB0, and mplib.h prototypes for mpLib_80055E24 and mpLib_800565DC, so call sites in these stage files can compile with more precise argument types.

Touched grlast.c with include ordering and many localized changes, but the provided context does not show a clear high-level behavioral intent beyond supporting the same stage-decomp/header cleanup wave.
smart_moves: Built stage callback tables first, then used them to drive typed callback setup; this mirrors how other ground stage files are structured and gives many small functions the correct HSD_GObj/Ground_GObj signatures.

Moved stage-local state out of anonymous padding and UNK_PARAMS into typed structs and headers, enabling later decomp work to reference gp->gv.icemt, gp->gv.icemt2, gp->gv.pura, and gp->gv.pura2 instead of raw offsets.

Kept matching-relevant assert strings and source line numbers visible in code such as __assert("gricemt.c", ...) and __assert("grpura.c", ...), which can be useful for reconstructing control flow and line directives in decomp work.

Used existing stage idioms repeatedly: Ground_801C14D0 to obtain stage gobjs, GObj_SetupGXLink with grDisplay_801C5DB0, HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 for update callbacks, grAnime_801C8138/grAnime_801C77FC for animation, and Ground_801C2FE0 for ground updates.

Typed external helper APIs only as far as needed for observed call sites, for example grZakoGenerator_801CAEB0(int, int), mpLib_80055E24(int), and mpLib_800565DC(int), rather than leaving all callers blocked behind UNK_PARAMS.
decomp_lessons: For stage files, matching often depends on reconstructing StageData and StageCallbacks arrays before individual functions become clean; callback table entries reveal expected function signatures and lifecycle roles.

Ground union growth is a common prerequisite for stage matching: once gp->gv.<stage> fields are named at the right offsets, many repeated JObj, timer, and state-machine functions become expressible in C.

When a function is still partially unknown, converting only known portions of the prototype is still valuable; this PR leaves some UNK_RET entries while typing many HSD_GObj, Ground, HSD_JObj, Vec3, float, int, and bool parameters.

Stage init routines commonly set stage_info flags, create a fixed set of ground gobjs by ID, call Ground_801C39C0/Ground_801C3BB4, and then apply stage-specific camera/background/enemy-generator setup.
Repeated stage segment callbacks can often be matched by cloning the same animation/JObj-lookup pattern with only map IDs or JObj indices changed.

Header cleanup can be a matching enabler rather than just polish: typed prototypes in gricemt.h/grpura.h enabled C bodies to call shared mpLib, grZakoGenerator, baselib, and ground helpers without placeholder signatures.
naming_conventions: Ice Mountain symbols use the existing grIceMt_ prefix for functions and grIm_ prefix for file-scope data, e.g. grIceMt_801F686C, grIm_803E4718, grIm_803E4800, and grIm_804D69F4.
Pura symbols use grPura_ for functions and grPu_ for file-scope data, e.g. grPura_80211E08, grPu_803E6800, grPu_803E6A3C, and grPu_803E6AA0.
Stage parameter/ground variable structs used descriptive stage names where known, such as IceMountainParams, grIceMt_GroundVars, grIceMt_GroundVars2, grPura_GroundVars, and grPura_GroundVars2.
Unknown struct members remained offset-style names like xC4, xC8, xD8, xF4, xAC, and xBC, preserving layout information without inventing semantic names.
Function address comments in headers were preserved in the project style, e.g. /* 1F6868 */ and /* 211E08 */, while signatures were made more precise.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Reconstructed static data including StageData, StageCallbacks, Vec3 arrays, S16Vec3 arrays, GXColor arrays, constants, and HSD_ImageDesc entries to satisfy data-section and reference matching needs.
Used explicit float constants such as 0.0, 0.8, 2.0, 30.0, -30.0, 3600.0, and stage constants like 0x16 and 0x11 in the stage data and camera/background logic.
Matched callback plumbing through function pointer checks: set gp->x1C_callback from callback3, call callback0 immediately, and attach callback2 through HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54.
Converted empty or stub callbacks to typed no-op functions with the correct HSD_GObj/Ground_GObj argument rather than leaving void(void), which matters for callback table compatibility.

Used known engine calls as anchors for matching: Ground_801C49F8, Stage_80225194, Ground_801C3260, Ground_801C2BA4, grZakoGenerator_801CAE04, mpLib_800580C8, HSD_Randi, HSD_JObjSetTranslate, Camera_SetBackgroundColor, and HSD_MObjSetToonTextureImage.

Some code in the excerpt still contains commented-out attempts and suspicious placeholders, so not every displayed body should be treated as a final semantic model; the reliable reusable tactic is the staged replacement of placeholders with typed, table-driven structure.
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text excerpt is a positive maintainer-style comment from ribbanya: "Good work, thanks."
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs touching gricemt.c to see which partially unknown Ice Mountain functions such as grIceMt_801F7A2C, grIceMt_801F8CDC, grIceMt_801F993C, and grIceMt_801FA6D8 were completed or renamed.
Look for later PRs touching grpura.c to verify whether the Pura/Pokefloats JObj, toon texture, and color-transition code received cleaner struct field names.
Search for grIceMt_GroundVars and grPura_GroundVars in later commits to see whether xC4/xC8/xD8-style fields gained semantic names.
Search for grZakoGenerator_801CAE04 and mpLib_80055E24 signature changes to confirm whether the temporary UNK_T/UNK_RET typings were refined after this PR.
Compare grPura_80211E08 and grIceMt_801F71E8 against other stage gobj creation helpers for a reusable callback setup template.
